entry.title: Software I use
entry.content:
List of software and services that help me simplify my tasks

Browser:

  • Mozilla Firefox
  • Safari

Email:

  • Protonmail for private communication
  • Microsoft Outlook Exchange for work

Notes:

  • Microsoft OneNote for structural notes (like lecture notes)
  • Standard Notes for private notes
  • Evernote for archiving webpages, receipts etc.

File syncing and cloud backup:

  • Dropbox
  • OneDrive for Business
  • nBox (from NUS)
  • Crypotmator (for encrypting sensitive data before uploading to the cloud)

Word processor:

  • Microsoft Word

Text Editor:

  • Visual Studio Code

Communication:

  • WhatsApp
  • Skype
  • Signal

Data Analysis and visualization:

  • Python
  • Matlab
  • Igor Pro

Reference Manager:

  • EndNote

Music:

  • iTunes Player
  • Spotify

Misc:

  • Git/GitHub (code versioning)
  • Tor Browser (anonymous browsing)
  • Proton VPN
  • Microsoft To Do (formerly Wunderlist)
  • Authy (2-Factor authentication)
  • Transferwise (currency exchange)
  • Pulse Secure (work VPN)
  • Microsoft Remote Desktop
  • Keka for macOS (archiving utility)
  • App Cleaner for macOS (uninstaller)
entry.summary: List of software and services that help me simplify my tasks

entry.title: Napolionica to Duino walk
entry.content:
We had a long Easter walk today. Starting from Opicina Obelisk to Duino.

More pics: http://bit.ly/duino-walk

entry.title: A trail walk at Val Rosandra

link.title: A trail walk at Val Rosandra

entry.content: We had an enchanting trail walk at Val Rosandra. It was a sunny day. There were nice panoramic views, with mountains in our left and right, and in front and in back too. We walked along the river Val Rosandra, and occasionally crossing back and forth. The water stream made some wonderful cavities over the years. Then there was a tall beautiful waterfall. At the end we had some refreshments at a home-like restaurant. We had drinks, salami with cheese, olives, and warm breads. It was a wonderful day deep in the nature and with good friends :)

entry.title: Good evening Trieste
entry.content: Sight of Trieste from Napoleonica after the sun went down in this northern Italian town. In fact, it’s bird’s eye view of three countries: Italia, Slovenia, and Croatia. The bright lights remind me of Diwali (the famous festival of light in India).

entry.title: Barcolana 2015 - Sail race, Trieste
entry.content:
Today was the 47th Barcolana. About 1700 sailing boats participated in today’s sailing festival. This is the most crowded sailing competition I have ever seen, maybe you remember the scene from the movie Troy. It was a magnificent event. I had a wonderful sight from Napoleonica, which is high in the mountains. There was a lot of crowd everywhere, in Opicina, Piazza Unita, Barcola, to watch the event. The winner of the competition was Robertissima III.

More pics - https://500px.com/pranab/galleries/barcolana

entry.title: Bárcola, Trieste
entry.content: A sunset from Bárcola on the other day! Taken on my phone while running.

entry.title: Marathon and a peek into Florence

link.title: Marathon and a peek into Florence

entry.content:
Yesterday was the 31st City marathon of Florence. It’s a great experience. Lots of runners (12,000+) and spectators (they cheered us). The city has several big cathedral, museum, and other historical structures. After all, the city of da Vinci. The marathon route covers the major touristic points. There is a river through the city, and we ran alongside it several times. You get a lot of memories to take with you after running 42.2 KM in such a wonderful place. Grazie Firenze!

And today morning I climbed the Bell tower (414 steps) and the top of the Duomo. Also walked and explored some parts of the city.

entry.title: Mission Mt. Misen
entry.content: A mini-hiking to Mt. Misen. It is the highest point of Miyajima island. There are pagodas, temples, water falls along the hiking trail, and spectacular views from the top. We visited inside of a Buddhist temple. There are many many idols/statues of Gods, their followers, and daemons on the way. The weather was beautiful, sunny. Clearly the visibility was more poor in the direction of Hiroshima from the island than towards the open sea.

entry.title: Bay of Miyajima
entry.content: We visited Miyajima today, one hour by train from Saijo. Then 15 minutes boat ride to the island. A wonderful place! Tens of islands, clean blue water, and enchanting nature all around.

entry.title: Moon rise over Trieste
entry.content:
“Don’t tell me the sky’s the limit when there are footprints on the moon.” ― Paul Brandt

This is today’s moonrise over the hills of Trieste. I mean, today’s Moon is so big that one can even see the footprints of Neil Amstrong :) Don’t you? :P
